item.title: Sohel seeks pardon, wife denies charges
item.description: Prime accused Sohel Rana on Wednesday begged the court’s pardon and his wife denied the charges against her while they had been defending themselves in a special tribunal in the case of killing an eight-year-old girl after rape at Pallabi in the capital on May 19......

item.title: IMF plans Dhaka visit over fresh loan deal
item.description: The International Monetary Fund on Wednesday said that plans were under way for a staff visit to Dhaka following a request from the newly elected government for a new financial arrangement......

item.title: Political parties decry power price hike
item.description: Different political parties on Wednesday protested against the government decision to increase power prices again, saying that it would deepen people’s sufferings......

item.title: Bangladesh may face additional US tariffs
item.description: The United States has proposed additional tariffs ranging from 10 per cent to 12.5 per cent on imports from Bangladesh and 59 other economies following investigations into the alleged failure of countries to prohibit and effectively enforce bans on the import of goods produced with forced labour......

item.title: Former Narayanganj mayor Salina Hayat Ivy released on bail after one year
item.description: Former Narayanganj City Corporation mayor Salina Hayat Ivy was released on bail from Kashimpur Central Women’s Prison minutes after 10:00pm on Wednesday after languishing in jails for one year in connection with 12 cases linked to 2024 mass uprising......

item.title: Bangladesh a win away from hat-trick title
item.description: Super-sub Sagorika scored a dramatic stoppage-time winner as Bangladesh came from a goal down to beat Nepal 2-1 in the semi-final and advance to the final of the SAFF Women’s Championship at the Jawaharlal Nehru Stadium in Goa, India, on Wednesday......

item.title: Mosaddek, Nurul return for Australia ODIs
item.description: Mosaddek Hossain returned to the national fold after four years as he was included in the squad for the first two ODIs against Australia, announced by the Bangladesh Cricket Board on Wednesday......

item.title: Yamal, Williams should be fit for WC opener: De la Fuente
item.description: Forwards Lamine Yamal and Nico Williams should be fit for Spain’s World Cup opener, coach Luis de la Fuente said on Wednesday......

item.title: Rupganj snatches thrilling win
item.description: Rupganj Tigers Cricket Club snatched a crucial win as they beat Bashundhara Strikers by one run in a curtailed game in the ongoing Dhaka Premier Division Cricket League at the Cricketers Academy on Wednesday......

item.title: Bangladesh fail to secure semi spots
item.description: Bangladesh’s men’s and women’s hockey teams failed to secure spots in the semi-finals of the Under-18 Asia Cup after losing to their respective rivals in their third and final pool matches at the Kawasaki Heavy Industries Hockey Stadium in Kakamigahara, Japan, on Wednesday......

item.title: Lukaku makes Belgium return with ‘ideal’ goal in win over Croatia
item.description: Belgium captain Youri Tielemans said on Tuesday Napoli forward Romelu Lukaku scoring on his first international appearance in more than a year in a 2-0 World Cup friendly win over Croatia was ‘ideal’......

item.title: A flawed remark that weakens Dhaka’s position
item.description: THE home minister’s remark that deaths resulting from border-related crimes or illegal crossing should not be labelled as ‘border killings’ unsettlingly echoes the legally and morally flawed Indian narrative that has for long sought to manipulate the definition of border killings while continuing to justify its brutal border management practices. The remark is also regrettably......

item.title: Trinamool leader’s allegation too serious to be ignored
item.description: The allegations that the former chief minister of West Bengal, also president of the Trinamool Congress, made at rally in Kolkata on June 2 warrant serious diplomatic attention. In her first public appearance after her defeat in the West Bengal legislative assembly elections in April, which ended her 15-year rule of the state, Mamata Banerjee alleged that India’s home minister......

item.title: Cartography of defascistisation
item.description: DEFASCISTISATION in Bangladesh means more than removing a ruling party or replacing one political elite with another. It requires dismantling the political culture of domination itself: the habit of concentrating power at the centre, policing dissent, and forcing society into a single moral or cultural mould. Bangladesh has already experienced a form of authoritarian......

item.title: Bangladesh at SDG crossroads
item.description: BANGLADESH’S development story is often told in linear terms: falling poverty, rising school enrolment, improved health outcomes, and steady progress towards the Sustainable Development Goals. On paper, the trajectory still appears broadly positive......

item.title: Unlocking Bangladesh’s night-time economy
item.description: THE night-time economy refers to the range of economic and social activities that continue between evening and early morning, including restaurants, cafés, transport services, retail trade, tourism, healthcare, logistics, cultural events, digital services and informal businesses. In many countries, these activities have evolved into major drivers of employment, urban......

item.title: Health budget needs transformative vision
item.description: THE health sector budget is unlikely to see a significant increase unless policymakers recognise that health is both the driving force and a precondition for national development. Physical and mental well-being underpin achievement in education, agriculture, industry, business and public administration. A healthy population is essential for productivity, sound decision-making, effective leadership and social stability......

item.title: Investigators struggle to trace previous IOs: AG
item.description: Attorney general Md Ruhul Quddus on Wednesday said that investigators were facing difficulties in tracing several key individuals who were involved in the initial stages of the investigation into the 2012 murders of journalist couple Sagar Sarowar and Meherun Runi......

item.title: 2 arrested over mugging of Mohammadpur sisters
item.description: The police have arrested two suspected snatchers in connection with the recent snatching of bags and other personal belongings from two sisters outside their residence in the capital’s Mohammadpur area......

item.title: Coast Guard arrests 9 while ‘smuggling’ cement to Myanmar
item.description: Members of the Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested nine smugglers along with a large amount of cement while smuggling it to Myanmar on Wednesday......

item.title: NCP alleges attack on Nasiruddin Patwary’s motorcade at Benapole
item.description: National Citizen Party alleged that a vehicle carrying its chief coordinator Nasiruddin Patwary and his associates came under attack in Benapole on Wednesday......

item.title: Teenage girl’s body found in sack in Pabna
item.description: The body of a teenage girl was found in a sack with her hands tied in the Padma River in Sadar upazila of Pabna on Wednesday......

item.title: Youth electrocuted in Gaibandha
item.description: A youth electrocuted while harvesting ripe mangoes at his father-in-law’s house in Gaibandha on Wednesday......

item.title: Vrathree Shangha distributes food items among 200 helpless families
item.description: The Vrathree Shangha has recently distributed food items among more than 200 helpless families on the occasion of Eid-ul-Azha in Dhaka......

item.title: SUF holds harmony feast
item.description: Voluntary organisation Swa-Udyogi Foundation has recently organised a harmony feast on the Holy Child International School campus at Mirpur 2 in Dhaka......

item.title: ‘No tangible progress’ in talks with US to end war: Iran FM
item.description: ‘Returning to the negotiating table is conditional on ensuring the rights of the Iranian people, ending the war in Lebanon, and stopping tensions in the region,’ Aragchi said....

item.title: PM’s foreign visit schedule solely his decision: Shama Obaed
item.description: State minister for foreign affairs Shama Obaed Islam on Wednesday urged the media not to give undue importance to reports regarding prime minister’s foreign visits until an official announcement is made by the government......

item.title: War drone kills three Myanmar migrants in Thailand
item.description: Three migrants from Myanmar have been killed and two wounded after a drone used in their country’s civil war exploded in neighbouring Thailand where they were working, Thai police said Wednesday......

item.title: Ukrainian drones hit Saint Petersburg as ‘Russian Davos’ opens
item.description: Ukrainian drones hit energy and military sites in Saint Petersburg on Wednesday as officials gathered for a flagship economic forum in the city, Russian and Ukrainian authorities said......

item.title: 32 artistes, technicians to receive National Film Awards 2023
item.description: The government has announced the National Film Awards 2023, selecting 32 artistes and technicians in 28 categories for their outstanding contributions to the country’s film industry......

item.title: UNO foils marriage of Class X student in Kushtia
item.description: A child marriage involving a Class X student was prevented by the local administration in a raid at Kumarkhali upazila of Kushtia in the small hours of Wednesday......

item.title: Youth arrested in Raiganj disabled child rape case
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion on Wednesday arrested a youth on charges of raping a Class II student with disabilities at Raiganj in Sirajganj......

item.title: Wasim Jabber joins IUB as treasurer
item.description: Md Wasim Jabber joined Independent University, Bangladesh as treasurer on Wednesday......

item.title: All English medium schools to operate under uniform policy: Bobby Hajjaj
item.description: State minister for primary and mass education on Wednesday said that all English medium schools will be brought under a single policy......

item.title: 50 hurt in Habiganj clash over ‘Argentina-Brazil’ football match
item.description: Over 50 people were injured in a clash between two groups in Sadar upazila of Habiganj on Wednesday following a dispute stemming from a local friendly football match between two teams named after Argentina and Brazil......

item.title: Man killed after being hit by train in Kushtia
item.description: A man died after a train ran over him while he was crossing railway tracks at Mirpur railway station in Kushtia on Wednesday......

item.title: 2 ‘drug dealers’ arrested with Yaba pills at Teknaf
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard arrested two drug peddlers with 15,000 Yaba pills worth Tk 75 lakh at Teknaf in Cox’s Bazar on Wednesday......

item.title: Trump says ‘would like to meet’ Mojtaba Khamenei
item.description: US president Donald Trump said Wednesday he hopes to meet Iran’s supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei, as Washington and Tehran accuse each other of violating an increasingly fragile ceasefire......

item.title: Iran, US trade fire, drone strike on Kuwait airport kills one
item.description: An Iranian drone strike on a passenger terminal at Kuwait’s international airport killed an Indian national and wounded 63 people on Wednesday, as conflict escalated between Tehran and US forces in the Gulf......

item.title: ‘Brought dead’ claim raises questions
item.description: Two of the six death certificates issued for newborns who died at Ad-Din Medical College Hospital on May 27 stated that the babies were brought dead to the hospital, citing cardiorespiratory failure as the cause of death......
